message.content:
1) Yes, a project screenshot image is visible and rendered (not broken/empty). It shows a POS terminal interface with menu items, categories, and a clean layout.  
2) All elements are visible and well laid out: the project name ("QuadShot POS"), tagline ("A café, run from one screen."), tech tags (React, JavaScript, CSS, Supabase, Vercel), and facts grid (role, year, deployment, database) are clearly displayed with proper spacing and alignment.  
3) No overlapping text, cut-off content, or layout bugs are present. The design is cohesive, with the screenshot and text sections balanced without visual clutter.  
4) The section looks cinematic and polished. The dark background contrasts well with the light screenshot, creating a professional, modern aesthetic. The typography, spacing, and visual hierarchy (e.g., bold project name, subtle tagline, organized tech tags) contribute to a refined, attention-grabbing presentation.
